title: Thin Terracotta Baked Indian Flour Pilaf
description: A light and fragrant Indian-inspired dish made with plain flour, perfect for a quick weeknight meal. This pilaf is free from common FODMAPs, making it a great option for those with digestive sensitivities.

ingredients.name: Mother Sauce Base (see Notes)
ingredients.notes: see notes section for mother sauce recipe

instructions: Heat the ghee or oil in a terracotta dish over medium-high heat.
Add the cumin seeds and cook for 1-2 minutes until fragrant.
Add the chopped onion and cook until lightly browned, about 5-7 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Stir in the plain flour and cook for 1-2 minutes until lightly browned.
Add the turmeric and salt to taste, and stir to combine.
Add 2 cups of water and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 10-15 minutes or until the pilaf is cooked through.
Stir in the mother sauce base, fresh cilantro, and lime juice.
Serve hot, garnished with additional cilantro if desired.
